accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context: EO 14323 of July 30, 2025, the Federal Register cite, section 202 of the National Emergencies Act, and the sponsor/committee details are correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the framing as terminating a trade-related national emergency on Brazilian imports is accurate context confirmed by the record.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill's purpose is to terminate the national emergency declared to impose duties on Brazilian imports.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the action is pursuant to section 202 of the National Emergencies Act (50 U.S.C. 1622) and terminates EO 14323.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or conditions, and the summary accurately notes their absence.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective date or timing provision for the termination, so this criterion does not apply.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect: termination of the EO 14323 national emergency.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, though the final sentence is cut off.

concise.why: Largely concise with minimal padding for such a short resolution.

correct_entities.why: Sponsor Gregory Meeks, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, EO 14323, and the National Emergencies Act are all named correctly.

correct_figures.why: Dates (July 30, 2025; February 2, 2026), the Federal Register cite (90 Fed. Reg. 37739), and the six co-sponsors count are all accurate.
